content: Keyset (cursor) pagination vs OFFSET/LIMIT: OFFSET pagination degrades linearly (O(n)) as offset grows because Postgres must still scan/skip the offset rows even with an index, and it has a correctness bug under concurrent inserts/deletes (rows shift between pages, causing skips/duplicates). Keyset pagination compares a compound cursor (e.g. (created_at, id) tuple) via WHERE (created_at, id) < (, ) ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC LIMIT , which always uses the index and gives stable results regardless of concurrent writes. Tradeoff: no random access to an arbitrary page number, and the client must carry the cursor from the last row of the previous page. For a notifications feed (sequential, newest-first, no page-number jumping needed), keyset pagination is the recommended approach and matches this codebase's stated goal of not repeating the existing unbounded-OFFSET tech debt.
